title: A Perfect Planet
subTitle: Without volcanoes, there would be no life on Earth. Although destructive, magma from the planet's molten core forms land and mineral-rich ash from eruptions fertilises the surface.

subTitle: Tibetan Qinghai Plateau is home to animals such as snow leopards and foxes who have adapted to survive in the rugged landscape of western China.

subTitle: There are numerous remote islands around the world that offer sanctuary for some of the planet's strangest and rarest creatures.

subTitle: David Attenborough narrates an icy adventure through the world's frozen zones, where wolves hunt, penguins huddle, and life thrives against the most extreme odds.

subTitle: While the coasts of Scandinavia are wild and unpredictable, it is also home to a wide rage of fauna like sea eagles, otters, base jumpers and orca.

title: Planet Earth III
subTitle: David Attenborough potrays the efforts of numerous conservation advocates who risk everything in order to rescue wildlife.

title: Big Little Journeys
subTitle: The filmmaker captures the journeys of two young creatures as one navigates towering forests in Canada in search of wetlands while the other follows distant lights in South Africa.

title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
subTitle: David Attenborough explores the continent of Antarctica and the challenges faced by its inhabitants due to changing climate.

title: Wild Alaska
subTitle: During springtime in Alaska, wilderness awakens and chaos ensues among various animals as they compete with each other for resources.

title: A Perfect Planet
subTitle: The host discusses various methods in which Earth and its natural resources like volcanoes, water bodies and other environmental phenomenons mould themselves in order to support life.

title: Nature's Greatest Dancers
subTitle: Steve Backshall explores the various animals, like ring-tailed lemurs and a tiny brown banana frog, that use amorous glances and tantalising moves in pursuit of romance.

title: The Hunt
subTitle: The contests between predators and prey are the most dramatic events in nature. Explore the extraordinary range of techniques employed by animals in order to trap their target.

title: Dynasties
subTitle: In Senegal, West Africa, live a group of chimpanzees, led by an alpha male named David. He has already been alpha for 3 years - a time when leaders here are usually overthrown. To make matters worse, David has no allies - no-one to help him.

title: Serengeti
subTitle: There's humour, heartbreak and nail-biting tension as our animal families face new tests and fresh rivalries. Soon a devastating storm creates the biggest problem of all.

title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
subTitle: The presenter explores Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs. Additionally, the weird and wonderful animals marooned in this place exist like nowhere else on Earth.

subTitle: The last leg of Joanna Lumley's journey takes her to Zanzibar, the spice island of Africa where merchants from around the world have left their mark.

subTitle: Gregg Wallace follows the production of pocket-sprung mattresses, from the arrival of hard steel to soft bedding heading out of dispatch, at a massive factory.

title: Chasing Monsters
subTitle: Cyril Chauquet is assigned the dangerous task of capturing an electric eel that is believed to emit a shock powerful enough to kill a human.

title: Earth's Sacred Wonders
subTitle: At the Church of the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem, an orthodox Christian must deliver a holy flame into the hands of his bishop; at the Boudhanath in Nepal, a Buddhist painter battles the elements in honor of Buddha's birthday.
